Home › Books › Inhuman Series

YA Dystopian Thriller · Ages 13+ · Grades 6-12

Inhuman Series

Cross the wall. Break every rule. A two-book duology where the line between human and animal no longer exists.

About the Series

The World of Inhuman

The Ferae Naturae virus swept through civilization like wildfire, splicing human DNA with animal DNA and turning millions into terrifying and sometimes beautiful hybrids called manimals. To protect the uninfected, the government erected the Titan wall, sealing the Feral Zone and everyone inside it away from humanity forever.

Lane McEvoy knows she will never cross that wall. Until she has to. When her father is taken into the Zone, Lane follows and discovers a world more complex, more dangerous, and more human than any quarantine map suggests.

Built on real virology and genetic science, with Kirkus and Booklist praise for its bizarre imagery and fast-paced mash-up of romance and horror.

The Real Science Inside

Inhuman draws on real zoonotic disease research, gene-virus interactions, and ecological collapse theory. It is ideal for NGSS connections in life science and bioethics discussions in grades 6-12.

Educator Resources →

Reading Order

  1. 1

    Inhuman

    September 24, 2013
  2. 2

    Undaunted

    March 26, 2019

Awards & Recognition

  • Kirkus starred review
  • Tome Society It List 2014-15
  • Truman Award nominee
  • Great Lakes Great Reads nominee
  • Approx. 18 language translations

Content Notes

Ages 13+. Kissing, mild profanity, suspense, and moderate creature and combat violence.

The Books

Inhuman

September 24, 2013 · 13+ · Grades 6–12

Years after the Ferae Naturae virus turned millions into half-human "manimals," Lane McEvoy is forced across the Titan wall into the Feral Zone — a quarantine wilderness where the infected roam. To save her father, she'll have to survive a world where the line between human and animal no longer exists.

Read More →

Undaunted

March 26, 2019 · 13+ · Grades 6–12

Six months after the events of Inhuman, Lane's fight to bridge the world inside the wall and the world outside it isn't over — it's only gotten more dangerous. The thrilling conclusion to the Inhuman duology.

Read More →
Kat Falls

NYT bestselling author of Dark Life and Inhuman.
Evanston, IL.
